original_abstract: Abstract In this study, a two pyrazole derivatives; 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide ( Pyz-1 ) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol ( Pyz-2 ) were synthesized and characterized by 13 C-NMR, 1 H-NMR, FT-IR, and mass spectrometry. A complete molecular structures optimization, electronic and thermodynamic properties of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 in gas phase and aqueous solution were predicted by using hybrid B3LYP method with the 6-311++G** basis sets. Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 were evaluated in vitro for their anti-diabetic, antioxidant and xanthine oxidase inhibition activities. For anti-diabetic activity, Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 showed a potent α-glucosidase and α-amylase inhibition with IC 50 values of 75.62 ± 0.56, 95.85 ± 0.92 and 119.3 ± 0.75, 120.2 ± 0.68 µM, respectively, compared to Acarbose (IC 50(α-glucosidase) = 72.58 ± 0.68 µM, IC 50(α-amylase) = 115.6 ± 0.574 µM). In xanthine oxidase assay, Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 exhibited remarkable inhibitory ability with IC 50 values 24.32 ± 0.78 and 10.75 ± 0.54 µM, respectively. The result of antioxidant activities showed that the title compounds have considerable antioxidant and radical scavenger abilities. In addition, molecular docking simulation was used to determine the binding modes and energies between the title compounds and α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes.
humanized_abstract: A study on antioxidant and antidiabetic activities of novel two pyrazole derivatives Abstract: Two novel two pyrazole derivatives viz., 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2) have been synthesized and characterized by 13 C-NMR, 1 H-NMR, FT-IR and mass spectrometry. The complete molecular structures optimization, electronic and thermodynamic properties of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 in gas phase and aqueous solution were predicted by using hybrid B3LYP method with the 6-311++G** basis sets. The title compounds Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 were evaluated for their antidiabetic, antioxidant and xanthine oxidase inhibition activities. For antidiabetic studies, both Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 showed potential inhibition towards α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes with IC50 values of 75.62 ± 0.56 and 95.85 ± 0.92 µM, 119.3 ± 0.75 and 120.2 ± 0.68 µM respectively. The IC50 values for both enzymes are compared with that of standard Acarbose, which showed IC50 values of 72.58 ± 0.68 µM and 115.6 ± 0.574 µM for α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes respectively. The inhibitory potential towards xanthine oxidase activity was studied by analyzing the IC50 values of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 towards the enzyme which showed that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 has excellent inhibitory activity towards the enzyme with IC50 values of 24.32 ± 0.78 and 10.75 ± 0.54 µM respectively. The antioxidants activity studies clearly indicate that the title compounds Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 have excellent scavenging and free radical activities. Molecular docking studies have been done between the title compounds and the α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes to check their binding modes and energies towards inhibition of enzyme activity.

The IC50 values for both enzymes are compared with that of standard Acarbose, which showed IC50 values of 72.58 ± 0.68 µM and 115.6 ± 0.574 µM for α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes respectively. The antioxidants activity studies clearly indicate that the title compounds Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 have excellent scavenging and free radical activities. Molecular docking studies have been done between the title compounds and the α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes to check their binding modes and energies towards inhibition of enzyme activity.
